observed_outcome.summary: Settings footer remains dirty after a successful save

plausible_prior_paths.hypothesis: Save succeeded; configUpdated rehydrated server config; routing-role select re-dirtied local draft because equality check did not compare the rehydrated value with the just-saved value
Server returned 200 but only persisted a subset; the unsaved field stays dirty

likely_split_point.description: Post-save config rehydration path does not deduplicate the just-saved value before triggering controlled-input re-dirty
likely_split_point.after_state: local draft differs by referential identity from saved config; controlled select fires onChange

damaged_invariants.invariant: A successful save should result in local draft state equal to saved state, with dirty flag cleared

minimal_repair_path.summary: Add an equality guard before re-dirtying the routing-role select when configUpdated rehydrates an identical value

steps.reason: Confirm where the controlled select onChange fires after rehydration

steps.action: Add equality guard: skip onChange when incoming rehydrated value === current draft value
steps.reason: Prevent re-dirty when rehydrated value is identical to the just-saved one

steps.action: Verify dirty flag clears after save and that legitimate user edits still mark dirty
steps.reason: Confirm the guard does not over-block real edits

uncertainty.topic: Whether other settings controls have the same re-dirty pattern
uncertainty.reason: Only the routing-role select was inspected in this trace

blocked_assumptions: Did not assume the server returned a stale payload without evidence
Did not assume the partial-persist warning applies to the routing-role field without confirming

notes: Worked example: dirty footer after save. Verdict restore_path_found with a low-cost candidate_patch step gated by Release Gate. Two competing prior paths preserved; high-confidence split point references concrete evidence; uncertainty about other controls explicitly recorded.
